perturbation.original_text: In the event that either party materially defaults in the performance of its duties or obligations set forth in this Agreement, and such default is not cured within thirty (30) days after written notice is given to the defaulting party specifying the default, then the party not in default may, by giving written notice thereof to the defaulting party, terminate the Agreement as of a date specified in such notice of termination.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that either party materially defaults in the performance of its duties or obligations set forth in this Agreement, and such default is not cured within fifteen (15) days after written notice is given to the defaulting party specifying the default, then the party not in default may, by giving written notice thereof to the defaulting party, terminate the Agreement as of a date specified in such notice of termination.
perturbation.explanation: The changed text reduces the cure period for a material default from thirty (30) days to fifteen (15) days. This introduces an in-text contradiction, since it changes the deadlines for the party to cure their defaults, leading to uncertainty on what term should be followed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that either party materially defaults in the performance of its duties or obligations set forth in this Agreement, and such default is not cured within fifteen (15) days after written notice is given to the defaulting party specifying the default, then the party not in default may, by giving written notice thereof to the defaulting party, terminate the Agreement as of a date specified in such notice of termination.
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the cure period for a material default from thirty (30) days to fifteen (15) days. The contradicted text also states that the cure period is fifteen (15) days. Although the contradicted text is consistent with the changed text, the fact that the original text states that the cure period is thirty (30) days causes a direct conflict, inconsistency, or contradiction within the document, since the document would then contain two different cure periods, creating uncertainty on what term should be followed. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding which terms should be enforced, leading to disputes over contractual obligations.
